text.headline: First mention of the Order of the Black Arrow
text.text: The term "Order of the Black Arrow" first appears in Ryu's posts on the Bay12 forums for the Doomforests succession fort. They are characterized as a secret society, operating within multiple fortresses and opposed to paladins led by Imic.

media.caption: First known post in the OBA

text.headline: Start of Counting
text.text: I start Counting based on a similar game run on the KSP forums. It would grow to become one of the most popular and contentious aspects of the server.

text.headline: Start of the Eternal Tower
text.text: Doubloon starts the Eternal Tower. Progress is slow at times, but as of this writing, over 5000 floors have been built.

text.headline: Start of Spelling
text.text: A new forum game similar to Counting, but for words. I quickly add mechanics of changing the season (and the rules) when the word gets stuck and punishing anyone who makes a swear word.

text.headline: Valley of Peace
text.text: A modded minecraft server including Create, Ars Noveau, and Cobblemon. All of us built our bases near each other in a valley, which included many constructions (some questionable) such as a Pokemon gym, the Todd Howard, and the fountain of blood. We attempted to do a quasi-hardcore system of giving players quests when they died, but this quickly petered out.

media.caption: The Valley of Peace in its final state

text.headline: Modded Hardcore
text.text: We played hardcore with some mods, most notably Origins, which leads to some very creative death messages. Every 5 worlds we did a fish world which was often complete nonsense.

media.caption: Only known screenshot of this period, from World 4. Todd is phased out using the phantom power I believe.

text.headline: "Dayvale"
text.text: With some help, Ryu figures out how to host a server on a spare PC of his. This server was home to many megaconstructions, such as the viaduct, Ryu's mountain castle, a library, the Cathedral of Tim, and a gas station.

media.caption: Constructing the viaduct using happy ghasts. Spawn area in a very early stage of construction visible in background.

text.headline: First speedrun
text.text: The OBA decides to try some Minecraft speedrunning and ends up with a time of 2:50:53. This will drastically improve in the future.

text.headline: First hardcore world
text.text: The beginning of the Hardcore multiplayer worlds, where we try to beat Minecraft but have to reset when anyone dies. The first world abruptly ends when Ryu and Block fall on a fence post, thus beginning the legend of Tim.

media.caption: Our base in the first hardcore world. The original Tim is barely visible to the left.

text.headline: Hardcore Minecraft
text.text:
The final death count stands at:
Ryu: 14.5
Blue: 4
Blackrock: 2
Kev: 1
Todd: 1
Alphadestroyer: 1
Blockmath: 0.5
The leading causes of death are: mobs (9), infighting (6), gravity (4), and drowning (2)

media.caption: Standard response to an attempt ending

text.headline: Hardcore victory (?)
text.text: The Hardcore gang finally defeats the ender dragon on World 25. I did die and had to be resurrected using a golden apple, so this still isn't a "true" victory.

text.headline: Amplified world
text.text: We played some vanilla Minecraft on an amplified world. Most of us build our houses around a lake, except Kev who finds a massive sky island far above spawn and builds his house on it. Sadly we never revisited this world past the first day.

media.caption: Central lake area, from the top of my lighthouse

text.headline: Wither fight world
text.text: A normal Minecraft server where we settled in a savanna near a lake, where we built a large enclosed area and Ryu keeps bees. Culminated in us challenging the Wither, which goes catastrophically wrong. The server is quietly abandoned in the aftermath.

media.caption: Ryu overlooking the central base area.

text.headline: Riviera SMP
text.text: A server with a small world border and different nations with lots of RP. Highlights include Ryu naming himself the Grey King and building on Moonrise Island, me building canals, Chron building an underwater secret base, and Imic building a magnificent underground fortress as he is wont to do. Had its own (now archived) channel, #riviera, which implies that the last activity is Todd playing alone on 8/15/22.

media.caption: Map of the server area at start, with some of my attempts at naming things

text.headline: Third Life
text.text: Inspired by the popular Youtube series, we attempted to run a Third Life server, where each player has three lives and may attack other players on their last life, there is a world border, and the server is only online on Saturdays. Honestly nothing really came of the idea, with the most notable events being me dying to creepers, Chron collecting cats, Ryu founding The Valley in the northwest corner, and Fal pursuing a strategy of only playing when no one is online and griefing the world with water walls and fire. Interest slowly died down to just Fal setting fires by 9/18/21.

media.caption: Water walls created by Fal, or the "topless towers of Fal's stupidity" as I deemed them

text.headline: Reign of Zero
text.text: After raising the count from -500 in under 2 months, Team Zero manages to hold the count near zero for almost an entire year, except for a slight perturbation when I remove the slowmode on July 4th.

text.headline: Great Channel Reduction
text.text: As an April Fools joke, Block reduces the OBA down to only 3 channels. Noticing that this appears to have increased activity by concentrating users into fewer channels, the admin team drastically reduces the number of channels, moving many obsolete or redundant channels into the archives.

media.caption: Logo of the OBA during the April Fools joke

text.headline: Elarifa Campaign begins
text.text: The OBA's first D&D campaign begins, with me DMing for Nindis the half-elven ranger (played by Ryu), Snezho the kobold rogue (played by Block), Leiraz the tiefling paladin (played by Kev). We are soon joined by Barkun Porter the human bard (played by Todd) and John Argentine the human warlock (played by JW). Along with their beloved horse, Gabe Newell, they form the Fellowship of the Cart.

text.headline: Camflain begins
text.text: Seeking a campaign to play when JW is absent, Kev starts running The Ocean Sleeps, a campaign set in Flain. The party consists of Lan the (blue) tortle druid (played by me), Briaric the fairy cleric (played by Ryu), gork the human paladin (played by Block), Thendri the vedalken artificer (played by Castel), and Torridon the water genasi druid (played by Todd).

text.headline: Lagoon server
text.text: This was a server where a lot of us settled around a beautiful lake. I mostly remember that I built an underground base together with Kev and Castel. This was also the server where Kev built the nuclear wasteland and the first Netro; we also had Creative Mode builds such as Canterlot.

media.caption: Dynmap screenshot of the central area near the end of the server, showing Canterlot, the lagoon, the wasteland, and Ryu's bridge

text.headline: December 2019 election
text.text: This is the oldest election attested in #balcony but there were earlier ones. At this point elections were for a single Chancellor to a 3-month term, who was one of four admins. Unfortunately the elections channel has been deleted so further details are not available.

text.headline: Ryu quits
text.text: After a period of personal turmoil and conflict with the other members of the server, Ryu resigns as owner, leaves the server, and is banned. This begins the Civil War / Splinter Server period. I believe MHB is in control at this point.

text.headline: Ryu reinstated
text.text:
In Ryu's words:
MHB had not been popular and many people had left for the splinter server over the months. He threatened to delete the OBA, or as it was called then, The Order of the Snek. But Codeman managed to talk him into handing ownership to him, then I talked to a group of which he was banned in and got him unbanned and he passed it back to me.

text.headline: Elections become annual
text.text: Elections are changed to being held once every year instead of 4 times a year. Apparently I was against this for some reason, probably because it meant that I could mess with the election 4x less often.

media.caption: #balcony post proposing the change. This is only a proposal, but as I recall it was implemented.

text.headline: Catgirl Chron lobby
text.text: In the September 2021 election, Ryu put all 4 admin positions up for election. He also imposed a 20 vote threshold for Chron specifically to win the election. As the deadline approached and it became clear that Chron was not going to reach the threshold, a group of members including Fallacy promised to vote for him if and only if he changed his profile picture to a catgirl for the next year upon his victory. He agreed and received the 20 votes necessary. The election was ultimately won by Chron, me, Imic, and TricMagic. It appears that future elections also put all 4 admin slots up for vote.

text.headline: June 2022 election
text.text: Last election I can find on the record. Among others, Inky runs on a platform of removing complex numbers from #counting, which she immediately makes good on.

media.caption: Announcement of the results. Note that "#BUREAU_OF_INFORMATION" is actually inky.exe, not me.

text.headline: End of elected admins
text.text:
Ryu begins attempting to thin the admin list, first by annoying some of us into quitting, then directly removing admins at Gabby's suggestion. Mention is made of removing Sanya, Isabelle, and Chron, to some frustration from both them and random members. Ryu may be playing some other minor pranks on the server at this point. I escalate things by hiding #forum and claiming I've deleted it, which gets me immediately de-admined, and the ordeal ends with elected admins removed.

It appears a lot of the actual announcements were deleted, so this is reconstructed from what remains and what I can recall.

media.caption: I have no image to represent this period yet; here is a mostly-unrelated quote as a placeholder

text.headline: Complex numbers in #counting
text.text: As a reward/punishment for the count being at zero for so long, I add complex numbers to counting. They remain until they are removed by inky.
